title: haplophyllum versicolor subsp. australe
intro: Haplophyllum versicolor subsp. australe is a plant species within the Rutaceae family, commonly known as the yellow-flowering or Australian yellow-flowering or yellow bush. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Haplophyllum versicolor subsp. australe can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
